title: Assign Azure RBAC at the narrowest scope with built-in roles
description: Bind a specific built-in role at the resource or resource-group scope rather than Owner/Contributor at subscription, respecting downward inheritance.
body: Use when granting Azure access or reviewing a role assignment. Do not assign Owner or Contributor at subscription scope to make something work, and avoid custom roles when a built-in role fits. Actions: pick the most specific built-in role (e.g. Storage Blob Data Reader, AcrPull), assign it at the resource or resource-group scope since RBAC inherits downward, prefer assignments to a managed identity or Entra group, and review with az role assignment list. Validation signals include the principal holding only the intended role at the intended scope and a denied action on a sibling resource. Risk boundary: any assignment at subscription/management-group scope or any Owner grant is human-gated.
